name: Lightning AI Studios API
what_it_does: Cloud ML development platform from PyTorch Lightning team with REST API for managing Studios (cloud dev environments with GPU), running training jobs, and orchestrating ML workflows — tightly integrated with PyTorch Lightning ecosystem.
use_cases: Launching GPU training jobs programmatically from MLOps agents
Managing persistent cloud development Studios with attached GPU instances
Running distributed PyTorch Lightning training across multiple GPUs via API
Monitoring training job progress and metrics for ML pipeline automation
Deploying Lightning-trained models as endpoints via the platform
not_for: Non-PyTorch ML frameworks — Lightning AI is deeply PyTorch-centric
Simple CPU workloads without GPU requirements
Organizations needing multi-cloud GPU flexibility without vendor lock-in
best_when: An ML team heavily using PyTorch Lightning needs integrated cloud development environments and training orchestration without managing Kubernetes.
avoid_when: You use TensorFlow or JAX primarily, or need multi-cloud GPU access — use Modal or Vast.ai instead.

auth.notes: API key from Lightning AI account. Used in Python SDK for authentication. Token-based for REST API calls. Organization-level and personal tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 22 GPU compute hours/month (T4), 2 free studios
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-use GPU compute: from $0.35/hour (T4) to $3.50/hour (A100); Teams plans
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for ML experimentation. GPU compute priced per hour. Studios persist between sessions. Competitive with Colab Pro for ML development.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; GPU availability may limit launch times

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python SDK is primary interface. REST API less documented than SDK. Studio and job management well-covered. Error responses include failure reasons. GPU availability can cause delays.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Studio state queries are idempotent. Job launches create new jobs each call. Studio creation with same name returns existing studio.
known_agent_gotchas: GPU availability varies — agents may need to wait for instance provisioning
Studios have auto-sleep behavior — ensure active before running jobs
PyTorch-centric ecosystem — less natural for non-PyTorch frameworks
Training job logs require polling or streaming — no push notifications

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 compliant. API key auth. TLS enforced. US data residency. GPU instances isolated per user. Secret management for training job environment variables.
